rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement As Being In Good Faith, And For Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Co., West American Insurance Co., Safeco Insurance Co. of America, Travelers Indemnity Co., Zurich American Insurance Co.¿s motion for court approval of settlement as being in good faith, and for dismissal is denied. Intervenors did not make a sufficient showing of all the Tech-Bilt factors in either its moving or reply papers. (Rutter Group Book on Civil Procedure Before Trial at 12:872-3). Specifically, intervenors did not provide sufficient evidence of a rough approximation of plaintiffs¿ total recovery for their wrongful death claims and a rough approximation of Rich-Tex¿s proportionate liability. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Company, West American Insurance Company, Safeco Insurance Company Of America, Travelers Indemnity Co., And Zurich American Insurance Co., Successor In Interest To Zurich Insurance Company, Us Branchs, Motion For An Order Severing Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Co., West American Insurance Co., Safeco Insurance Co. of America, Travelers Indemnity Co., Zurich American Insurance Co.¿s motion for order severing cross-complaint is denied without prejudice to renewing the motion if all parties do not agree on a continuance of the trial for a sufficient period of time to enable intervenors to be able to adequately prepared for trial. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Imai, Tadlock, Keeney & Cordery¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for The C.P. Hall Co. is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to give proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. § 1005(b). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SUMNER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Pulis was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Purported undisputed material fact 12 in defendant¿s separate statement states that the deposition testimony of James Dorris is the ¿only case-specific¿ witness or document identified by plaintiffs in their contention discovery responses that Ms. Pulis was exposed to asbestos from a product attributable to defendant. Among the evidence cited to support this purported undisputed fact is exhibits B and C to Mr. Stewart¿s declaration submitted with the moving papers. Page 4 lines 1 and 2 of Exhibit C show that the purported undisputed fact 12 is not correct. In those lines plaintiffs identify two persons (Jack Davis and Tommy Peck) who are nowhere discussed in defendant¿s separate statement. While it may be that defendant has made reasonable efforts to locate and contact Mr. Davis and Mr. Peck and been unsuccessful, there is no evidence to that effect. If the tentative ruling is confirmed, the court will hear argument on defendant¿s application to continue the trial date. = (220/HEK)
